frameworks.name: ethological neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: studying neural computations through species-typical, evolutionarily shaped behaviours

frameworks.name: naturalistic behaviour platform
frameworks.one_line: open-source habitat integrating navigation, nesting, escape, foraging, and social modules

frameworks.name: continuous long-term behavioural monitoring
frameworks.one_line: multi-mouse tracking from sub-millisecond to month-long timescales

core_question: How can self-guided ethological behaviours and neural activity be captured continuously in customizable multi-mouse habitats?
key_assumptions: ethological behaviours reveal robust evolved neural computations
transitory naturalistic behaviours require long-term, large-arena observation
experimental control is compatible with naturalistic settings
manipulations: environmental conditions in a changing habitat
task assays such as digging-to-threshold and social foraging
key_findings: platform enables multi-dimensional quantification across sub-ms to month timescales
mice adapt strategies to changing environments
mice adjust behaviour in response to conspecifics during social foraging
interpretations: naturalistic, continuous assays expose ethological behaviours inaccessible to standard tasks
modular open-source habitats can combine experimental control with ethological richness
